etymology.description: 廾(廾) (gǒng, "both hands") + 午(午) (wǔ, "pestle") + 臼(臼) (jiù, "mortar") = using both hands to pound with a mortar and pestle -> pound grain
etymology.interpretation: The character 舂(chōng, "pound grain") visually represents the action of pounding grain using both hands with a pestle and mortar, reflecting its agricultural context and function.
